records.topicTitle: SBRT for medically inoperable stage I NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: SBRT is recommended as the non-surgical treatment of choice for stage I NSCLC [II, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use for medically inoperable stage I disease; also recommended in severe COPD, elderly and/or frail patients.

records.topicTitle: Sublobar resection for peripheral stage I tumors ≤2 cm or pure ground-glass lesions
records.sourceExcerptShort: Sublobar resection is recommended for peripherally located, resectable tumours ≤2 cm with no nodal metastases; it can be recommended for pure ground-glass lesions [I, A]/[III, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Peripheral resectable tumour ≤2 cm with CT/PET node-negative disease, or pure ground-glass lesion; anatomical resection preferred over wedge when feasible.

records.topicTitle: Lobectomy for operable stage I tumors >2 cm with solid appearance
records.sourceExcerptShort: Lobectomy can be recommended as the standard surgical treatment of tumours >2 cm that have a solid appearance on CT [II, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Operable stage I disease; VATS or RATS recommended where appropriate; systematic nodal dissection should accompany surgery.

records.topicTitle: Salvage surgery after local progression following SBRT
records.sourceExcerptShort: Salvage surgery after SBRT can be offered, if feasible, using the same indications as for primary surgery [IV, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Applies after local recurrence or new primary following SBRT in a patient who remains operable; higher operative risk should be acknowledged.

records.topicTitle: PORT after R1 resection
records.sourceExcerptShort: PORT (60 Gy in 30 fractions) may be recommended after R1 resection, following MDT discussion [III, C].
records.applicabilityNotes: Sequence relative to adjuvant systemic therapy should be individualized by MDT; record intended for incompletely resected curative-intent cases.

records.topicTitle: Do not use PORT after R0 resection
records.sourceExcerptShort: PORT cannot be recommended after R0 resection, including for patients with extracapsular nodal involvement if margins are negative [I, D].
records.applicabilityNotes: Applies when surgical margins are negative after complete resection.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant osimertinib for resected EGFR-mutated stage IB NSCLC

biomarkerLogic.notes: Restrict to sensitising EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R.

records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant osimertinib for 3 years is recommended for completely resected EGFR-mutated (exon 19 deletion or L858R) stage IB-IIIA NSCLC [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: For stage IB, applies particularly to tumours >3 cm or other high-risk features; use after chemotherapy as appropriate.

records.topicTitle: Neoadjuvant nivolumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for resectable EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant nivolumab–ChT is recommended for resectable stage II-III NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use in ChT–ICI-eligible resectable stage II-III disease; FDA/EMA approval note for PD-L1 TC ≥1% should be handled operationally if regulatory filtering is needed.

records.topicTitle: Perioperative pembrolizumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for resectable EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab–ChT followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab is recommended for resectable stage II-III NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use in ChT–ICI-eligible resectable stage II-III disease.

records.topicTitle: Perioperative durvalumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for resectable EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant durvalumab–ChT followed by adjuvant durvalumab is recommended for resectable stage II-III NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use in ChT–ICI-eligible resectable stage II-III disease.

records.topicTitle: Perioperative nivolumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for resectable EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant nivolumab–ChT followed by adjuvant nivolumab is recommended for resectable stage II-III NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use in ChT–ICI-eligible resectable stage II-III disease; FDA/EMA approval note for PD-L1 TC ≥1% should be handled operationally if regulatory filtering is needed.

records.topicTitle: Perioperative tislelizumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for resectable EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant tislelizumab–ChT followed by adjuvant tislelizumab is recommended for resectable stage II-III NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use in ChT–ICI-eligible resectable stage II-III disease; EMA approved and not FDA approved at guideline publication.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy for completely resected EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC not eligible for ChT–ICI
records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant cisplatin-based ChT is recommended for completely resected EGFR and ALK wild-type stage II-III NSCLC not eligible for ChT–ICI [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use after R0 resection when perioperative/neoadjuvant chemo-immunotherapy is not used or not suitable.

records.topicTitle: Carboplatin-based adjuvant chemotherapy when cisplatin is not suitable
records.sourceExcerptShort: Carboplatin-based regimens can be recommended for patients who are not eligible for cisplatin [II, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use for renal, neurological or other cisplatin contraindications.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant atezolizumab after platinum chemotherapy for PD-L1-positive resected EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-IIIA NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant atezolizumab for 1 year is recommended following cisplatin-based ChT for resected stage II-IIIA (T ≥5 cm) NSCLC with PD-L1 TC ≥1% and without EGFR or ALK alterations [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use after cisplatin-based adjuvant chemotherapy; EMA approval was limited to PD-L1 TC ≥50% at publication, while FDA approval covered PD-L1 TC ≥1%.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant pembrolizumab after platinum chemotherapy for resected EGFR/ALK wild-type stage II-IIIA NSCLC regardless of PD-L1
records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant pembrolizumab for 1 year is recommended for resected stage II-IIIA (T ≥4 cm) NSCLC without EGFR or ALK alterations, regardless of PD-L1 expression [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use after platinum-based chemotherapy.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant osimertinib for completely resected EGFR-mutated stage II-IIIA NSCLC

biomarkerLogic.notes: Restrict to sensitising EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R.

records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant osimertinib for 3 years is recommended for completely resected EGFR-mutated (exon 19 deletion or L858R) stage IB-IIIA NSCLC [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: For stage II-III disease, cisplatin-based chemotherapy before targeted therapy is recommended when the patient is chemotherapy-eligible.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy before osimertinib in EGFR-mutated stage II-III NSCLC

biomarkerLogic.notes: Restrict to sensitising EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R.

records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant cisplatin-based ChT before targeted therapy is recommended for patients with stage II-III EGFR-mutated disease who are eligible for ChT [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use before adjuvant osimertinib; carboplatin-based regimens can be considered when cisplatin is unsuitable.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant alectinib for completely resected ALK fusion-positive stage II-IIIA NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant alectinib for 2 years is recommended for completely resected ALK fusion-positive stage II-IIIA (T ≥4 cm) NSCLC [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Applies to resectable ALK-rearranged disease; molecular testing should be performed preoperatively.

records.topicTitle: Adjuvant chemotherapy before targeted therapy in ALK fusion-positive stage II-IIIA NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Adjuvant ChT before targeted therapy may be considered for ALK-rearranged disease [II, C].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use before adjuvant alectinib when the MDT judges additional platinum-based chemotherapy appropriate.

records.topicTitle: Neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by surgery for superior sulcus tumors
records.sourceExcerptShort: Neoadjuvant CRT followed by surgery can be recommended for patients with superior sulcus tumours (T3-4 N0-1) after MDT discussion [III, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use only after MDT review in potentially resectable superior sulcus disease.

records.topicTitle: Concurrent chemoradiotherapy for unresectable stage III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Concurrent CRT is recommended for patients with stage IIIA-C NSCLC deemed unresectable by an MDT [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: RT dose should be 60 Gy in once-daily fractions; use platinum-based doublet chemotherapy for two to three cycles.

records.topicTitle: Sequential chemoradiotherapy or radiotherapy alone for unresectable stage III NSCLC when concurrent CRT is not suitable
records.sourceExcerptShort: In patients who are not fit for concurrent CRT, sequential CRT or RT alone is recommended as an alternative [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use for frail patients or those with substantial comorbidities that make concurrent CRT unsuitable.

records.topicTitle: Platinum-based doublet chemotherapy backbone for unresectable stage III NSCLC treated with CRT
records.sourceExcerptShort: Concurrent or sequential platinum-based doublet ChT for two to three cycles is recommended [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Preferred regimens: cisplatin–etoposide or carboplatin–paclitaxel for all histologies; cisplatin–pemetrexed for non-squamous histology.

records.topicTitle: Consolidation osimertinib after CRT for EGFR-mutated unresectable stage III NSCLC without progression

biomarkerLogic.notes: Restrict to sensitising EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R.

records.sourceExcerptShort: Osimertinib consolidation is recommended after concurrent or sequential CRT in patients with EGFR-mutated unresectable stage III NSCLC without disease progression [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Use after either concurrent or sequential CRT; based on LAURA, primarily sensitising exon 19 deletion or L858R disease.

records.topicTitle: Consolidation durvalumab after concurrent CRT for EGFR wild-type and PD-L1 ≥1% unresectable stage III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Durvalumab consolidation for 1 year (initiated within 42 days after the end of CRT) is recommended after concurrent CRT in patients with EGFR wild-type stage III NSCLC without disease progression [I, A].
records.applicabilityNotes: Figure 3 restricts this branch to EGFR wild-type and PD-L1 ≥1%; regulatory note: EMA approval required PD-L1 TC ≥1% at publication.

records.topicTitle: Consolidation durvalumab after sequential CRT for EGFR wild-type and PD-L1 ≥1% unresectable stage III NSCLC
records.sourceExcerptShort: Durvalumab consolidation for 1 year can also be recommended after sequential CRT in patients with EGFR wild-type stage III NSCLC without disease progression [III, B].
records.applicabilityNotes: Figure 3 restricts this branch to EGFR wild-type and PD-L1 ≥1%; EMA approved for PD-L1 TC ≥1%, not FDA approved at publication.

records.topicTitle: Avoid consolidation durvalumab in unresectable stage III EGFR-mutated NSCLC

biomarkerLogic.notes: Based on expert consensus after lack of clear PACIFIC benefit and toxicity concerns when TKIs follow immunotherapy.

records.sourceExcerptShort: An ESMO expert consensus statement recommended avoiding durvalumab in patients with unresectable stage III EGFR-mutated NSCLC following concurrent CRT.
records.applicabilityNotes: Use osimertinib consolidation instead for sensitising EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R disease without progression after CRT.
